message:
fix: convert OG images from WebP to PNG to fix satori crash Satori (the JSX→SVG renderer inside next/og's ImageResponse) does not support WebP images. When it encounters a WebP data URI in an , its internal resolveImageData returns undefined, which then gets spread/iterated, causing "TypeError: u2 is not iterable" during static generation of per-page opengraph-image routes. Root cause confirmed by: - Identical root OG code (which worked) failed when placed in sub-routes - Removing WebP background from sub-route OGs made them pass - The root OG appeared to work due to build ordering/caching quirks Fix: - Pre-convert all 6 WebP background screenshots to 1200px-wide PNG variants (suffixed _og.png, 90-234KB each) using sharp - Update all 9 OG image files (root, twitter, 7 per-page) to reference PNG data URIs instead of WebP - Replace borderRadius: "full" (invalid CSS for satori) with "9999px" - Replace
tags (unsupported by satori) with separate elements using display: "flex" and flexDirection: "column" - Add display: "flex" to all elements per satori requirements - Restore beads and architecture OG images to their full rich designs (were simplified during debugging) All 25 routes now build successfully including all 8 opengraph-image routes and the twitter-image route. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6
